claim_bullets.bullet_text: Copies properties into a new object (e.g., Object.assign({}, {a:1})) (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.198-205)
Extends an existing object with another object's properties (Object.assign(inventory, shipment)) (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.198-205)
Copies properties from one object to another using a for (let fruit in shipment) { inventory[fruit] = shipment[fruit] } (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.198-205)
Augments an instance's own properties via Object.assign(this,{array:[],head:0,tail:-1}) (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.198-205)

Defines a Queue constructor and prototype methods by assigning to this and Queue.prototype with Object.assign; shallow copy may not duplicate nested functions, **possible** (raw/javascriptallonge.pdf p.198-205)

source_record_text: Object.assign is a standard function that assigns properties from source objects to a target object, enabling shallow copying, object extension, and prototype augmentation.
